other_observers: Dan Zmolek, Leslie Sutton, Gwen Robeson and many others.
description: The bird was a cormorant, which was significantly smaller than the Double-crested Cormorants that were nearby. It had a much smaller body and bill, but also showed a quite long tail: as least as long as those on the D-c Cormorants.  It had a small white area at the back of the bill, which extended down a very small distance below the bill. It had a conspicuous green eye. The neck showed some brown feathers and was not totally black, which is why I think it was an immature bird that hadn't quite reached adult plumage.
photographer: Photographer: Leslie Sutton, Leslie Sutton, Thomas Heinrich, Thomas Heinrich
